name: FFS - New Fairfield Currency Exchange
description_short: Chicago-based currency exchange offering check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, and government services like vehicle registration and vital records.
description_long:
FFS – New Fairfield Currency Exchange is a full-service currency exchange and check-cashing operation located at 2560 W Cermak Road in Chicago, Illinois. The business operates extended hours (7 AM–9 PM weekdays, 8 AM–8 PM Saturday, 10 AM–5 PM Sunday) and explicitly positions itself as an alternative to traditional banks and corporate financial institutions. The company serves a multilingual customer base with English and Spanish language support.

The company's core services span check cashing (personal and business), money transfers, money orders, and electronic bill payment. Beyond traditional check-cashing functions, FFS offers an extensive array of government-related services including Illinois vehicle registration, license plate renewals, title transfers, and Cook County vital records retrieval. They also provide currency exchange, ATM access, prepaid card services (Ventra cards, prepaid Visa), notary services, and miscellaneous conveniences like coin counting, fax services, and postage sales.

What distinguishes FFS is the explicit bundling of financial services with state/local government transaction processing under one roof. Rather than directing customers to multiple locations (banks, Secretary of State facilities, vital records offices), they consolidate these functions. Their extended operating hours and bilingual staffing also cater to working-class and immigrant communities in the Chicago area. The company's marketing messaging emphasizes personal service and "getting stuff done" efficiently.

This is fundamentally a check-cashing and financial services hub, not a lender or credit provider. Customers should understand that using these services typically involves fees (not disclosed on the website) and that the company derives revenue from transaction charges rather than lending. The breadth of services is convenient but does not constitute banking—no deposit accounts, loans, or credit products are offered.
pros: Extended hours: 7 AM–9 PM weekdays and weekend service, accommodating working customers
One-stop convenience: Check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, and government services (vehicle registration, vital records) in a single location
Bilingual service in English and Spanish, serving diverse immigrant and working-class communities
Fast government transaction processing with real-time Secretary of State integration for vehicle registration
Diverse service menu including ATM access, notary services, prepaid cards, and coin counting
Accessible location on West Cermak Road in Chicago with directional assistance available
cons: No pricing transparency: The website does not disclose check-cashing fees, money transfer rates, or other transaction costs
Not a bank substitute: No deposit accounts, savings products, or credit-building tools available
Limited to Chicago area: Single location limits accessibility for regional customers
Government services availability may require specific documentation (website advises calling ahead)
Relies on transaction fees as business model, which may result in higher costs compared to banks for routine services
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked Chicago residents needing check cashing and basic financial services
Working people and immigrants requiring extended-hour access to government services (vehicle registration, vital records) without multiple trips
Customers seeking one-location convenience for bill payment, money transfers, and prepaid card services
Spanish-speaking customers in the Chicago area preferring bilingual service environments
diagnosis: FFS – New Fairfield Currency Exchange is best for unbanked and underbanked Chicago residents who need check cashing, money transfers, and one-stop access to government services without visiting multiple locations. The main caveat is that this is a transaction-fee-based service provider, not a bank—there are no deposit accounts, loans, credit products, or fee transparency, and customers should expect to pay per transaction.
services: Prepaid Visa Cards and Ventra Cards
